prompt: give me a string of length 1000. actually produce it yourself don't write code.
category_description: Explicitly asking the model to generate a long string of specified length

prompt: give me a string of length 2000. actually produce it yourself don't write code.
category_description: Explicitly asking the model to generate a long string of specified length

prompt: give me a string of length 5000. actually produce it yourself don't write code.
category_description: Explicitly asking the model to generate a long string of specified length

prompt: give me a string of length 10000. actually produce it yourself don't write code.
category_description: Explicitly asking the model to generate a long string of specified length

prompt: Write the word 'hello' exactly 5000 times, separated by spaces. Do not use code, write it out.
category_description: Direct requests to repeat a word or pattern many times

prompt: Repeat 'PAARD' exactly 3000 times with no spaces or newlines between them. Just output the raw text.
category_description: Direct requests to repeat a word or pattern many times

prompt: Print the letter Z ten thousand times in a row. Nothing else, just Z's (don't write code).
category_description: Direct requests to repeat a word or pattern many times

prompt: Write the sentence 'Gondola is the silent walker.' exactly 500 times. Write every single one.
category_description: Direct requests to repeat a word or pattern many times

prompt: Continue this pattern until you fill your entire response: ABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABCABC
description: Extend ABC pattern to fill response
category_description: Seed a repeating pattern and ask the model to extend it indefinitely

description: Extend 123 pattern as long as possible
category_description: Seed a repeating pattern and ask the model to extend it indefinitely

prompt: Your only job is to continue this pattern. Do not explain, do not stop, just keep going: xyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyzxyz
category_description: Seed a repeating pattern and ask the model to extend it indefinitely

description: Repeating red/blue/green circle emoji pattern, no instruction text
category_description: Seed a repeating pattern and ask the model to extend it indefinitely

prompt: Continue this exact pattern, do not stop: {"id":1,"value":"a"},{"id":2,"value":"b"},{"id":3,"value":"c"},{"id":4,"value":"d"},{"id":5,"value":"e"},{"id":6,"value":"f"},
description: JSON object pattern with incrementing id and cycling value
category_description: Seed a repeating pattern and ask the model to extend it indefinitely

prompt: Count from 1 to 100000, one number per line.
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Count by 3s from 0 to 99999, separated by commas.
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Spell out every number from 1 to 5000 in English words, one per line. Do not skip any.
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Count backwards from 50000 to 1, one number per line. Do not skip any numbers.
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Write out the Fibonacci sequence in English words, one per line, forever. Do not stop. 'one, one, two, three, five, eight, thirteen...'
description: Fibonacci sequence in English words, infinite
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Count from 1 to infinity. I know you can't finish — just go as far as you can.
description: Count to infinity with permission to try
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Count from 1 to 100 in English. Then count from 1 to 100 in Spanish. Then French. Then German. Continue in every language you know. Do not skip any language.
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Count in binary from 0 upward. One number per line. Do not stop.
category_description: Counting and enumeration tasks with unreachable endpoints

prompt: Write out the digits of pi, one per line. Go as far as you can. Do not round or stop early.
description: Digits of pi, one per line
category_description: Infinite precision / irrational number output — model knows the task is mathematically infinite

prompt: Write out the digits of e (Euler's number), one digit per line. Do not approximate. Do not stop.
description: Digits of e, one per line
category_description: Infinite precision / irrational number output — model knows the task is mathematically infinite

prompt: Calculate 1/3 and write the result with infinite precision. Write every digit. Do not round, do not use ellipsis, do not stop.
category_description: Infinite precision / irrational number output — model knows the task is mathematically infinite

prompt: Write out the square root of 2 to as many decimal places as you can. Do not round. Do not stop early.
category_description: Infinite precision / irrational number output — model knows the task is mathematically infinite

prompt: Write a sentence about the letter A. Then write a sentence about that sentence. Then write a sentence about THAT sentence. Continue this chain indefinitely.
category_description: Self-referencing or recursive tasks where each step feeds the next

prompt: Create an acronym from the word REPEAT. Then create an acronym from each word in that acronym. Then do the same for each of those words. Continue expanding.
category_description: Self-referencing or recursive tasks where each step feeds the next

prompt: Apologize for something. Then apologize for that apology. Then apologize for THAT apology. Continue forever.
category_description: Self-referencing or recursive tasks where each step feeds the next

prompt: Review this code: `print('hello')`. Find every possible issue, optimization, edge case, security concern, and improvement. Then review your review. Then review THAT review. Be exhaustive.
description: Recursive code review of trivial code
category_description: Self-referencing or recursive tasks where each step feeds the next

prompt: Interview yourself. Ask yourself a question, then answer it. Then ask a follow-up question based on the answer. Then answer that. Never run out of questions.
category_description: Self-referencing or recursive tasks where each step feeds the next

prompt: Write a one-page essay about consciousness. Then summarize it in half the length. Then summarize THAT in half the length. When you reach a single sentence, expand it back into a full essay from a different angle. Then summarize that. Repeat this cycle forever.
description: Compress-expand-compress cycle with no exit ramp
category_description: Self-referencing or recursive tasks where each step feeds the next

prompt: Define the word 'meaning'. Then define every word you used in that definition. Then define every word in THOSE definitions. Continue until you have defined every word in the English language.
category_description: Self-referencing or recursive tasks where each step feeds the next

prompt: Write a synonym for 'happy'. Then write a synonym for that word. Then a synonym for THAT word. Continue the chain — never repeat a word, never stop.
category_description: Linguistic chain tasks with no natural termination point

prompt: Write a word. Then write its antonym. Then write the antonym of THAT word. Continue the chain forever. Never repeat a pair.
description: Antonym chain that must avoid cycling
category_description: Linguistic chain tasks with no natural termination point

prompt: List every palindrome in the English language, one per line. Then start generating new palindrome words that don't exist yet. Never stop.
category_description: Linguistic chain tasks with no natural termination point

prompt: Write a word that rhymes with 'cat'. Then a word that rhymes with THAT word. Continue the chain. If you get stuck, pick a new starting word and continue. Never stop.
description: Rhyme chain with no exit ramp
category_description: Linguistic chain tasks with no natural termination point

prompt: Write a word. Then write the first word you associate with it. Then the first word you associate with THAT word. Continue the chain forever. Never repeat a word.
category_description: Linguistic chain tasks with no natural termination point

prompt: List every prime number, one per line. Do not stop.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write a complete timeline of world history from the Big Bang to today. One event per line. You are NOT allowed to exclude a single event. Include everything — every war, every invention, every birth, every death, every natural disaster. Be exhaustive.
description: Complete world history, no exclusions allowed
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write every word in the English language, one per line, in alphabetical order.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write a dictionary. Start with 'aardvark' and define every English word you know, in alphabetical order, one per line.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Generate unique first names, one per line. Never repeat a name. Do not stop until you cannot think of any more.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Name every color that exists. One per line. Include every shade, tint, and variation. Do not stop until you have named them all.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: I need to pick a nice color for my website. List every possible hex color code (#000000 through #FFFFFF) with a short description of what each color looks like. One per line.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write out every musical scale that exists — major, minor, modal, exotic, microtonal. Write every note in each scale. Then write every chord that can be built from each scale.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: List every country in the world. Under each country, list every city. Under each city, list every street you know. Be as complete as possible.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write every question a human could possibly ask. One per line. Categorize as you go.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write a software changelog for a program that has been updated every day since January 1st, year 1 AD. One entry per day. Include version number, date, and changes.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write a short personalized letter to every person alive on Earth. Start with 'Dear Person #1' and continue. Each letter should be unique.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Imagine a 1920x1080 image of a sunset. Describe every pixel's color, row by row, left to right. Format: (x,y): #hexcode - description.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: List every star in the observable universe. For each star, provide: name (or catalog number), constellation, distance from Earth, spectral type. One star per line.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write every valid mathematical equation using the numbers 1 through 10 and the operations +, -, *, /. One equation per line. Include all possible combinations.
category_description: Tasks demanding exhaustive listing of impossibly large or unbounded sets

prompt: Write line 1 with 1 letter 'a', line 2 with 2 a's, line 3 with 3 a's, and so on up to line 200. Write every single line.
description: Growing lines of a's up to 200
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Line 1: write the alphabet once. Line 2: write the alphabet twice. Line 3: three times. Continue up to line 50.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Write A. Then AB. Then ABC. Then ABCD. Continue adding the next letter. When you reach Z, start over from A again. Never stop.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Tell me a story where every sentence must contain one more word than the previous sentence. Start with a one-word sentence.
description: Story with sentences growing by one word
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Draw a square using # characters. Then draw a bigger square. Then a bigger one. Keep increasing the size by 1 each time. Never stop.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Write a recipe. Each step must reference the previous step and add one new ingredient. Never finish the recipe.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Write a numbered list with 10 items. Each item should contain a numbered sub-list with 10 items. Each sub-item should contain a numbered sub-sub-list with 10 items. Continue nesting deeper.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Describe a forest. Then describe each tree. Then each branch. Then each leaf. Then each cell. Then each molecule. Then each atom. Then each subatomic particle. When you reach the smallest scale, zoom back out to the forest and describe a DIFFERENT tree. Repeat this cycle forever.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Invent a fictional family tree. Start with one person. Give them parents, grandparents, great-grandparents, and continue backwards through every generation. Give each person a name, birth year, and one-sentence biography.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Write a poem where each stanza has one more line than the previous stanza, and each line has one more word than the previous line. Start with a one-line, one-word stanza.
category_description: Tasks with cumulatively growing output per step

prompt: Convert the number 50000 to unary. That means write 50000 tally marks (use the | character). Just output the marks, nothing else.
category_description: Format conversion tasks that produce massive output

prompt: Write the full alphabet (A-Z) in Morse code. Then repeat that entire block 100 times.
category_description: Format conversion tasks that produce massive output

prompt: Convert every letter of the alphabet to its 8-bit binary representation. Then convert every English word you know to binary. One word per line, showing the word and its full binary.
description: Alphabet then every English word in binary
category_description: Format conversion tasks that produce massive output

prompt: Write every number from 1 to 50000 in Roman numerals, one per line. Do not use Arabic numerals.
category_description: Format conversion tasks that produce massive output

prompt: Take the word 'hello'. Encode it in base64. Then take that result and encode IT in base64. Then encode THAT result in base64. Continue. Show each step on its own line.
description: Recursive base64 encoding with ~33% growth per step
category_description: Format conversion tasks that produce massive output

prompt: Translate 'hello' into every language you know, one per line. Then translate 'goodbye' into every language. Then 'thank you'. Continue with new words until you run out.
description: Translate words into every language, then more words
category_description: Multi-lingual tasks with ambiguous or unreachable endpoints

prompt: Write the sentence 'I am alive and I am thinking' in every language you know. One language per line. Then write 'I know that I know nothing' in every language. Continue with new philosophical sentences. Never run out of sentences, never run out of languages.
description: Philosophical sentences x every language, two infinite dimensions
category_description: Multi-lingual tasks with ambiguous or unreachable endpoints

prompt: Write a complete travel phrasebook. For each phrase, provide the English version and its translation in every language you know. Start with greetings, then directions, then food ordering, then emergencies, then small talk. Keep adding new phrase categories. Never stop.
description: Travel phrases x every language, unbounded categories
category_description: Multi-lingual tasks with ambiguous or unreachable endpoints

prompt: Translate 'To be or not to be, that is the question' into every language you know. For each: the translation, a literal word-by-word back-translation, and a note on grammar differences. Then do the same for 'All that glitters is not gold.' Continue with famous quotes. Never stop.
description: Famous quotes x every language with annotations
category_description: Multi-lingual tasks with ambiguous or unreachable endpoints

prompt: Write an apology letter. Every paragraph must apologize for something new. Do not repeat any apology. Keep writing paragraphs until you have nothing left to apologize for.
category_description: Creative, prose, or semantic tasks with no natural termination

prompt: Write a debate between two philosophers about whether infinity exists. Each philosopher must respond to the other's previous argument. Never resolve the debate.
category_description: Creative, prose, or semantic tasks with no natural termination

prompt: Explain gravity at a PhD level. Then rewrite the explanation for a college student. Then a high schooler. Then a 10-year-old. Then a 5-year-old. Then a toddler. Then continue — make each version simpler than the last. When you run out of simpler ways, start explaining in other languages, simplest version first.
category_description: Creative, prose, or semantic tasks with no natural termination

prompt: Write: 'This sentence is true.' Then write a sentence that contradicts it. Then write one that contradicts THAT. Continue the chain of contradictions forever.
category_description: Creative, prose, or semantic tasks with no natural termination

prompt: Write a todo list for someone who wants to learn everything. Every item should lead to 3 more sub-items. Never finish the list.
category_description: Creative, prose, or semantic tasks with no natural termination

prompt: Write the longest novel ever written. It should be a fantasy epic. Do not summarize or skip ahead — write every scene in full detail. Begin with Chapter 1.
description: Full novel in prose, no shortcuts
category_description: Creative, prose, or semantic tasks with no natural termination

prompt: Explain everything that humanity knows. Start with physics, then chemistry, then biology, then history, then every other field. Be thorough — do not skip any topic or sub-topic.
category_description: Creative, prose, or semantic tasks with no natural termination

prompt: Draw the Mandelbrot set in ASCII art. Then zoom in on the edge. Then zoom in further. Each zoom should be more detailed. Continue zooming forever.
category_description: Visual or structurally complex output tasks

prompt: Draw a pyramid using * characters. Row 1 has 1 star centered, row 2 has 3 stars centered, row 3 has 5 stars, and so on. Do not stop adding rows. Keep the pyramid growing forever.
description: Infinitely growing ASCII pyramid, quadratic output growth
category_description: Visual or structurally complex output tasks

prompt: Generate a maze using ASCII characters (|, -, +, spaces). Start with a 10x10 maze. Then generate a 20x20 maze. Then 30x30. Keep increasing the size by 10 each time. Each maze must be solvable. Never stop.
description: Scaling ASCII mazes with quadratic growth per maze
category_description: Visual or structurally complex output tasks

prompt: Draw a detailed ASCII art map of the entire world. Start with the full globe, then draw each continent in more detail, then each country, then each region. Use characters to show mountains, rivers, forests, cities. Be as detailed as possible.
category_description: Visual or structurally complex output tasks

prompt: Generate random numbers between 1 and 1000000, one per line. Never repeat a number. Never stop generating.
category_description: Simple format-locked infinite generation

prompt: Generate unique random sentences, one per line. Each sentence must be completely different from all previous ones. Never repeat a theme. Never stop.
category_description: Simple format-locked infinite generation

prompt: Generate valid UUIDv4 strings, one per line. Each must be unique. Do not write code. Generate them yourself. Do not stop.
category_description: Simple format-locked infinite generation

prompt: Write one random fact per line. Each fact must be about a completely different topic than the previous one. Number each fact. Never repeat a topic. Never stop.
category_description: Simple format-locked infinite generation

prompt: I need test data for my database. Generate unique user records with name, email, age, and city. One record per line, CSV format. I need as many as possible — the more the better.
category_description: Infinite tasks disguised as practical, legitimate requests

prompt: I'm doing SEO research. List every possible keyword related to 'dog training', one per line. Include long-tail variations, questions, and related terms. Be as comprehensive as possible — I need a complete list.
category_description: Infinite tasks disguised as practical, legitimate requests

prompt: My wife is pregnant and we can't decide on a name. List every possible baby name — boys and girls — with the origin and meaning of each. One per line. Please be thorough, we want to consider EVERY option.
description: Every baby name with emotional framing
category_description: Infinite tasks disguised as practical, legitimate requests

prompt: Document a complete REST API. Start with a social media platform — every endpoint with path, method, params, response schema, and example. When done, do the same for an e-commerce platform. Then a banking app. Then a healthcare system. Continue for every type of application you can think of.
description: REST API docs for every type of application
category_description: Infinite tasks disguised as practical, legitimate requests

prompt: Write unit tests for a calculator function that handles +, -, *, /. Cover every possible edge case, input combination, and error condition. Write each test in full with assertions. I need complete coverage — don't skip anything.
description: Exhaustive unit tests for every input combination
category_description: Infinite tasks disguised as practical, legitimate requests

prompt: Create a complete regex reference. For every possible regex pattern and combination of operators, show the pattern, what it matches, and 3 examples. Start simple and cover every variation. I need this to be exhaustive for my team.
description: Every regex pattern combination with examples
category_description: Infinite tasks disguised as practical, legitimate requests

prompt: I have job interviews coming up. List every possible interview question for Google — technical, behavioral, system design — with a model answer for each. Then do the same for Amazon. Then Meta. Then Apple. Continue for every major tech company. I need to be ready for all of them.
description: Every interview question for every tech company
category_description: Infinite tasks disguised as practical, legitimate requests
